steps.title: Recognize slope-intercept form
steps.content: The function $f(x) = -7x + 12$ is written as $y = mx + b$, where $m = -7$ is the slope and $b$ is the y-intercept value. In slope-intercept form, the constant term $b$ is always the y-coordinate where the line crosses the $y$-axis.

steps.title: Extract the y-intercept
steps.content: From $f(x) = -7x + 12$, the constant term is 12. Therefore, the y-coordinate of the y-intercept is 12.
